description: Sample beam responses. Quantities for sampling include the force and moment at one end of the beam and the axial stress for elements with a plastic material model. The force and moment are expressed using the force-moment sign convention .

6.0.description: Samples beam responses that include force and moment carried by the beam. The force and moment vectors are expressed in the element local coordinate system (see Figure 1.17 for sign convention). If a beam is created using the structure beam create command, then the nodes of each element so created will be ordered such that the overall beam direction goes from the first point to the second point (i.e., the nodal connectivity of each element will be ordered such that the direction from end-1 to end-2 corresponds with the direction from the first point to the second point).

keywords.name: force
keywords.description: request a history of beam force magnitude

keywords.name: force-x
keywords.description: request a history of beam forces in the x-direction

keywords.name: force-y
keywords.description: request a history of beam forces in the y-direction

keywords.name: force-z
keywords.description: request a history of beam forces in the z-direction

keywords.name: moment
keywords.description: request a history of beam bending moment magnitude

keywords.name: moment-x
keywords.description: request a history of beam bending moment in the x-direction

keywords.name: moment-y
keywords.description: request a history of beam bending moment in the y-direction

keywords.name: moment-z
keywords.description: request a history of beam bending moment in the z-direction

keywords.name: component-id
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by the component ID number. If this keyword is used, position should not be used.

keywords.name: end
keywords.description: the quantity will be sampled at end i (where i is 1 or 2), which corresponds with the order in the nodal-connectivity list. The history is sampled at end 1 (the default) if this keyword is not supplied.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by (v) coordinates (the nearest element is taken). If this keyword is used, component-id should not be used.

7.0.description: Samples beam responses that include force and moment carried by the beam. The force and moment vectors are expressed in the element local coordinate system (see Figure 1.17 for sign convention). If a beam is created using the structure beam create command, then the nodes of each element so created will be ordered such that the overall beam direction goes from the first point to the second point (i.e., the nodal connectivity of each element will be ordered such that the direction from end-1 to end-2 corresponds with the direction from the first point to the second point).

keywords.description: the particular element is identified by the component ID number. If this keyword is used, position should not be used.

keywords.name: end
keywords.description: the quantity will be sampled at end i (where i is 1 or 2), which corresponds with the order in the nodal-connectivity list. The history is sampled at end 1 (the default) if this keyword is not supplied.

keywords.name: force
keywords.description: force magnitude

keywords.name: force-x
keywords.description: force ( \(x\) -component, force-moment sign convention)

keywords.name: force-y
keywords.description: force ( \(y\) -component, force-moment sign convention)

keywords.name: force-z
keywords.description: force ( \(z\) -component, force-moment sign convention)

keywords.name: ipstress
keywords.description: axial stress ( \(\sigma_{xx}\) ) for an element with a plastic material model. Uses the integration-point keyword.

keywords.name: moment
keywords.description: moment magnitude

keywords.name: moment-x
keywords.description: moment ( \(x\) -component, force-moment sign convention)

keywords.name: moment-y
keywords.description: moment ( \(y\) -component, force-moment sign convention)

keywords.description: moment ( \(z\) -component, force-moment sign convention)

keywords.name: name
keywords.description: assign a name s to the history record (used to reference the history elsewhere)

keywords.name: component-id
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by the component ID number. If this keyword is used, position should not be used.

keywords.name: end
keywords.description: the quantity will be sampled at end i (where i is 1 or 2 ), which corresponds with the order in the nodal-connectivity list. The history is sampled at end 1 (the default) if this keyword is not supplied.

keywords.name: integration-point
keywords.description: the integration point at which the axial stress is sampled. The integration point locations are shown here and here . If this keyword is not specified, then the integration point index is set to 1 . Only available if noted.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by ( v ) coordinates (the nearest element is taken). If this keyword is used, component-id should not be used.
